name: World Sport
description: From the World Cup to the Olympics — test your knowledge of sport around the globe!
author: Game Night Crew

questions.question: Which country hosted the 2010 FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: That was us, nè? South Africa made history as the first African nation to host the World Cup. Ke nako!

questions.question: How many rings are on the Olympic flag?
questions.explanation: Five rings representing the five continents. If you got this wrong, you might need to watch more sport!

questions.question: In tennis, what is the term for a score of 40-40?
questions.explanation: Deuce! Both players are tied at 40-40, and someone needs to win two consecutive points to take the game.

questions.question: Which basketball player is nicknamed 'King James'?
questions.explanation: LeBron James, the King himself. Love him or hate him, the man can play basketball!

questions.question: Which sport uses a shuttlecock?
questions.explanation: Badminton! The shuttlecock (or birdie) can travel over 400 km/h in professional play. That's faster than most things on the N1!

questions.question: Which swimmer has won the most Olympic gold medals of all time?
questions.explanation: Michael Phelps with a mind-blowing 23 Olympic golds. The man is basically a fish with legs!

questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cup titles?
questions.explanation: Brazil with 5 World Cup titles! They make it look lekker easy sometimes, hey.

questions.question: What is the maximum break score possible in a single frame of snooker?
questions.explanation: A maximum break of 147 — all reds potted with blacks, then all the colours. Pure class!

questions.question: Which Grand Slam tennis tournament is played on clay courts?
questions.explanation: The French Open (Roland Garros) is the only Grand Slam on clay. Rafael Nadal basically owned that place!

questions.question: In Formula 1, which driver holds the record for most World Championship titles?
questions.explanation: Lewis Hamilton and Michael Schumacher are tied on 7 titles each, but Hamilton's 2020 title made him joint record holder. Legends!

questions.question: Which country won the first-ever Cricket World Cup in 1975?
questions.explanation: The West Indies won the inaugural Cricket World Cup in 1975, and again in 1979. Those Caribbean boys could play, nè?

questions.question: Who scored the 'Hand of God' goal in the 1986 FIFA World Cup?
questions.explanation: Diego Maradona punched the ball into the net against England. Cheeky, but he also scored what many call the greatest goal ever in the same match!

questions.question: How many players are on a volleyball team on the court at one time?
questions.explanation: 6 players per side in volleyball. And yes, beach volleyball only has 2 — much harder to hide!

questions.question: Which golfer has won the most major championships?
questions.explanation: Jack Nicklaus with 18 majors! Our own Gary Player won 9 — not too shabby for a Saffa, nè?

questions.question: Put these Olympic host cities in chronological order (earliest to latest):
questions.explanation: Athens 2004, Beijing 2008, London 2012, Rio 2016. The Olympics travels the world — maybe one day it'll come back to Africa!

questions.question: Which boxer was known as 'The Greatest' and famously said 'Float like a butterfly, sting like a bee'?
questions.explanation: Muhammad Ali — the man was as good with words as he was with his fists. An absolute legend of the ring!

questions.question: In which year did Usain Bolt set the 100m world record of 9.58 seconds?
questions.explanation: Bolt ran 9.58 seconds at the 2009 World Championships in Berlin. Lightning fast! Our own Wayde van Niekerk knows a thing or two about world records too.

questions.question: Which country has won the most Rugby World Cup titles?
questions.explanation: South Africa with 4 titles (1995, 2007, 2019, 2023)! The Springboks are the kings of rugby, and don't let anyone tell you otherwise!

questions.question: Which NBA team did Michael Jordan spend most of his career with?
questions.explanation: MJ and the Chicago Bulls — 6 championships together. His Airness made basketball the global sport it is today!

questions.question: What is the distance of a marathon in kilometres (approximately)?
questions.explanation: 42.195 km to be exact! The Comrades Marathon in SA is nearly double that — those runners are absolutely mad, in the best way.

questions.question: Which footballer has scored the most goals in FIFA World Cup history?
questions.explanation: Miroslav Klose with 16 World Cup goals, beating Ronaldo's (the Brazilian one) record of 15. What a career!

questions.question: In which sport would you perform a 'triple axel'?
questions.explanation: Figure skating! The triple axel is 3.5 rotations in the air. Imagine trying that on the ice rink at Canal Walk!

questions.question: Which country has won the most Olympic gold medals in history?
questions.explanation: The United States dominates the all-time Olympic gold medal count with over 1,000 golds. They're not shy about it either!

questions.question: Who holds the record for the most centuries in international cricket?
questions.explanation: Sachin Tendulkar with 100 international centuries — an insane achievement! Our own Jacques Kallis wasn't far behind with 62 though, and he bowled too!

questions.question: Which team has won the most UEFA Champions League titles?
questions.explanation: Real Madrid with 15 Champions League titles and counting. They just collect them like braai invites on a Saturday!

questions.question: In which Olympic sport do competitors use a foil, épée, or sabre?
questions.explanation: Fencing! Three weapons, three disciplines. It's basically a sword fight with rules and fancy outfits.

questions.question: Which tennis player has won the most Grand Slam singles titles (men's)?
questions.explanation: Novak Djokovic with 24 Grand Slam titles. The GOAT debate in tennis is like the braai vs barbecue debate — everyone has an opinion!

questions.question: What was the final score of the 2023 Rugby World Cup final between South Africa and New Zealand?
questions.explanation: The Boks won 12-11 in a nail-biter in Paris! Handre Pollard's penalty sealed it. The whole of Mzansi was going absolutely crazy!

questions.question: Arrange these FIFA World Cup winners in chronological order (earliest to latest):
questions.explanation: France 1998, Italy 2006, Spain 2010, Germany 2014. Four different countries, four lekker tournaments!

questions.question: Which city hosted the first modern Olympic Games in 1896?
questions.explanation: Athens, Greece — going back to the roots of the ancient Olympics. It only made sense!

questions.question: In cricket, what is a 'hat-trick'?
options: Bowling 3 maiden overs in a row
questions.explanation: Three wickets in three consecutive balls! It's one of the most exciting things in cricket. Kagiso Rabada knows the feeling!

questions.question: Which Formula 1 circuit is known as 'The Temple of Speed'?
questions.explanation: Monza in Italy — home of the Italian Grand Prix and one of the fastest tracks on the calendar. Those long straights are something else!

questions.question: Who was the first person to run a sub-4-minute mile?
questions.explanation: Roger Bannister broke the 4-minute mile barrier in 1954 with a time of 3:59.4. People thought it was impossible before that!

questions.question: Which country dominates international table tennis, having won the most Olympic golds in the sport?
questions.explanation: China absolutely dominates table tennis. They've won 32 of the 37 Olympic gold medals in the sport. Eish, that's proper domination!

questions.question: In which sport is the 'Ryder Cup' contested?
questions.explanation: The Ryder Cup is golf's biggest team event — Europe vs USA every two years. The atmosphere is unlike anything else in golf!

questions.question: Which heavyweight boxer retired undefeated with a 49-0 record in 1956?
questions.explanation: Rocky Marciano — the only heavyweight champion to retire undefeated. 49 fights, 49 wins, 43 by knockout. That's a lekker record!

questions.question: What is the 'Duckworth-Lewis-Stern' method used for in cricket?
questions.explanation: DLS is used to calculate revised targets when rain interrupts a limited-overs match. It's maths that makes even accountants confused, but it works!

questions.question: Arrange these athletics world records by how recently they were set (most recent first):
questions.explanation: Van Niekerk's 400m record was set in 2016 (in Rio, making SA proud!), Bolt's 100m in 2009, Powell's long jump in 1991, and Flo-Jo's 100m way back in 1988!

questions.question: Which country has won the most Hockey (field hockey) World Cup titles in men's competition?
questions.explanation: Pakistan has won the men's Hockey World Cup 4 times. They were dominant in the 70s and 80s — proper lekker hockey!

questions.question: In which year did the 'Miracle on Ice' occur, where the USA ice hockey team defeated the Soviet Union at the Olympics?
questions.explanation: The 1980 Winter Olympics in Lake Placid. A bunch of college kids beat the mighty Soviet Union! It's one of the greatest upsets in sport history.

questions.question: What is the only Grand Slam tennis tournament played on grass?
questions.explanation: Wimbledon! Strawberries, cream, and grass courts. It's the most traditional of all the Slams, and they still enforce a white dress code.

questions.question: Which athlete won 4 gold medals at the 1936 Berlin Olympics, embarrassing the Nazi regime?
questions.explanation: Jesse Owens won 4 golds right in front of Hitler. Actions speak louder than words, nè? One of sport's most powerful moments.

questions.question: In cycling, what is the name of the leader's jersey in the Tour de France?
questions.explanation: The famous Maillot Jaune! The yellow jersey has been the symbol of the race leader since 1919. Every cyclist dreams of wearing it in Paris.

questions.question: Which sport features the 'Fosbury Flop' technique?
questions.explanation: Dick Fosbury revolutionised the high jump at the 1968 Olympics by going over the bar backwards. Everyone thought he was crazy — until he won gold!

questions.question: Which is the only country to have competed in every single Summer Olympic Games since 1896?
questions.explanation: Greece has been at every Summer Olympics since 1896. As the birthplace of the Olympics, it would be rude not to show up! Note: Australia, France, and Great Britain have also competed in all editions.

questions.question: In which sport is a 'stone' delivered towards the 'house' on ice?
questions.explanation: Curling! Players slide a granite stone down ice towards a target. It looks easy until you try it — then you realise why they sweep so furiously!

questions.question: Arrange these sports in the order they were introduced to the Summer Olympics (earliest first):
questions.explanation: Tennis debuted in 1896, basketball in 1936, taekwondo in 2000, and skateboarding in 2020 (Tokyo). The Olympics keeps evolving!

questions.question: Which cricketer holds the record for the fastest century in ODI cricket (off just 31 balls)?
questions.explanation: AB de Villiers smashed 100 off just 31 balls against the West Indies in 2015. Mr. 360 — one of our proudest exports! That innings was absolutely bonkers.

questions.question: Which country won the first-ever Olympic gold medal in rugby sevens at the 2016 Rio Olympics?
questions.explanation: Fiji won their first-ever Olympic medal of any kind — and it was gold! The whole country celebrated. Rugby sevens made its Olympic debut that year and Fiji were absolutely dominant.

questions.question: In the sport of jai alai, what is the name of the curved basket used to catch and throw the ball?
questions.explanation: The chistera (also called cesta) is the curved wicker basket strapped to the player's hand. Jai alai is one of the fastest ball sports in the world — the ball can travel over 300 km/h!
